thag12.2:1.1: Into a lowly family I was born,
thag12.2:1.2: poor, with next to no food.
thag12.2:1.4: I gathered the spoiled, the withered flowers from shrines and threw them away.
thag12.2:3.1: Then I saw the One Self-awakened,
thag12.2:3.2: arrayed with a squadron of monks,
thag12.2:3.3: the Great Hero, entering the city,
thag12.2:4.2:
I approached him to do reverence.
He—the supreme man—stood still
thag12.2:4.3: out of sympathy just for me.
thag12.2:5.1: After paying homage to the feet of the teacher,
thag12.2:5.3: & requested the Going Forth from him,
thag12.2:6.2: sympathetic to all the world, said:
thag12.2:7.1: Alone, I stayed in the wilds,
thag12.2:7.4: just as he, the Conqueror, had taught me.
thag12.2:8.1: In the first watch of the night,
thag12.2:9.3: Indra & Brahmā came to pay homage to me,
thag12.2:10.1: “Homage to you, O thoroughbred of men,
thag12.2:10.2: Homage to you, O man supreme,
thag12.2:10.4: You, dear sir, are worthy of offerings.”
thag12.2:11.2: arrayed with a squadron of devas,
thag12.2:12.3: That’s how one is a brahman.
